name: Warren Buffett
title: Berkshire Hathaway CEO
description: The Oracle of Omaha and chairman of Berkshire Hathaway. Known for his long-term buy-and-hold value investing philosophy, he built one of the largest conglomerates in the world by acquiring undervalued companies with strong competitive moats. His annual shareholder letters are considered essential reading for investors worldwide.

name: Bill Ackman
title: Pershing Square Capital
description: Billionaire activist investor and founder of Pershing Square Capital Management. Known for taking concentrated positions in large-cap companies and pushing for operational changes to unlock shareholder value. Made headlines with his $2.6B profit on a COVID market hedge and high-profile battles with companies like Herbalife and Valeant.

name: Michael Burry
title: Scion Asset Management
description: The investor who predicted and profited from the 2008 subprime mortgage crisis, famously portrayed in The Big Short. Runs Scion Asset Management with a deep-value, contrarian approach. Known for making large concentrated bets against market consensus and frequently rotating his entire portfolio between quarters.

name: Ray Dalio
title: Bridgewater Associates Founder
description: Founder of Bridgewater Associates, the world's largest hedge fund managing approximately $100B in total assets. Pioneer of the 'All Weather' and 'Pure Alpha' strategies that combine systematic macro investing with risk parity allocation.

name: Cathie Wood
title: ARK Invest CEO
description: Founder and CEO of ARK Invest, known for her high-conviction bets on disruptive innovation including AI, genomics, robotics, and blockchain. Her flagship ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) became one of the most popular actively managed ETFs. Takes bold positions in early-stage technology companies and publishes her trades daily for full transparency.

name: David Tepper
title: Appaloosa Management Founder
description: Founder of Appaloosa Management, one of the most successful hedge fund managers in history. Specializes in distressed debt and equity investing with a contrarian macro approach. Known for making massive bets during market crises — his fund returned 132% in 2009 by buying beaten-down bank stocks.

name: George Soros
title: Soros Fund Management Founder
description: Legendary macro investor and founder of Soros Fund Management. Most famous for 'breaking the Bank of England' in 1992 by shorting the British pound, netting over $1 billion in a single day. His reflexivity theory of markets influenced a generation of traders.

name: Carl Icahn
title: Icahn Enterprises
description: One of Wall Street's most feared activist investors for over four decades. Built his fortune through hostile takeovers and corporate restructuring, pushing companies to unlock shareholder value through management changes, spin-offs, and buybacks.

name: Stanley Druckenmiller
title: Duquesne Family Office
description: Former lead portfolio manager for George Soros's Quantum Fund, where he co-executed the famous British pound trade. Later ran Duquesne Capital Management with one of the best long-term track records in hedge fund history — averaging 30% annual returns over 30 years with no losing year.

name: Seth Klarman
title: Baupost Group Founder
description: Founder of The Baupost Group and author of 'Margin of Safety,' one of the most sought-after investing books ever written. A disciple of Benjamin Graham's value investing philosophy, he focuses on finding deeply undervalued securities with a significant margin of safety.

name: Dan Loeb
title: Third Point LLC
description: Founder of Third Point LLC, an activist hedge fund that combines deep fundamental research with shareholder activism. Known for writing forceful public letters to company boards demanding strategic changes.

name: David Einhorn
title: Greenlight Capital Founder
description: Founder of Greenlight Capital (now filing as DME Capital Management), famous for publicly shorting Lehman Brothers months before its 2008 collapse. A disciplined value investor and accomplished poker player, he combines long positions in misunderstood businesses with high-conviction shorts against what he sees as accounting fictions.

name: Howard Marks
title: Oaktree Capital Co-Founder
description: Co-founder of Oaktree Capital Management, the world's largest distressed-debt investor. His investment memos are read across Wall Street — Warren Buffett says when one arrives, it's the first thing he opens. Known for market-cycle wisdom and buying quality assets when others are forced to sell.

name: Nelson Peltz
title: Trian Partners Founder
description: Founding partner of Trian Fund Management and one of the most prominent activist investors of his generation. Takes large stakes in iconic consumer and industrial companies — P&G, Heinz, Disney — and pushes for board seats and operational overhauls, a style he calls 'constructivism'.

name: Paul Singer
title: Elliott Management Founder
description: Founder of Elliott Investment Management, widely considered the most feared activist investor in the world. Famous for a 15-year legal battle with Argentina over defaulted bonds that ended with a $2.4B settlement, and for activist campaigns that have reshaped companies from AT&T to Salesforce.

name: Chris Hohn
title: TCI Fund Management Founder
description: Founder of The Children's Investment Fund (TCI), one of the most profitable hedge funds in history by dollar gains. Runs a highly concentrated portfolio of dominant infrastructure-like businesses and pairs aggressive shareholder activism with large-scale philanthropy.

name: Terry Smith
title: Fundsmith Founder
description: Founder of Fundsmith and often called 'the English Buffett'. His strategy is famously simple: buy good companies, don't overpay, do nothing. Runs a concentrated portfolio of high-return consumer and healthcare compounders held for years, and his blunt annual letters have a cult following.

name: Li Lu
title: Himalaya Capital Founder
description: Founder of Himalaya Capital and the only outside manager Charlie Munger ever trusted with his family's money. Introduced Berkshire to BYD, one of its most successful investments ever. Runs an ultra-concentrated value portfolio and is often called 'the Chinese Warren Buffett'.

name: Mohnish Pabrai
title: Pabrai Investment Funds Founder
description: Founder of Pabrai Investment Funds (filing as Dalal Street) and author of 'The Dhandho Investor'. An unapologetic 'cloner' of great investors' ideas, he famously paid $650K with Guy Spier for a charity lunch with Warren Buffett. Runs a highly concentrated low-fee value portfolio.

name: Bill Gates
title: Gates Foundation Trust
description: The Microsoft co-founder's philanthropic endowment, managed by his long-time investment chief Michael Larson. One of the most-watched 13F portfolios in the world — a concentrated collection of long-term holdings including Microsoft, Berkshire Hathaway, and Waste Management.

name: Chuck Akre
title: Akre Capital Founder
description: Founder of Akre Capital Management and creator of the 'three-legged stool' framework: great business, great management, great reinvestment. His fund holds compounding machines like Mastercard, Moody's, and O'Reilly for a decade or more with famously low turnover.

name: Thomas Gayner
title: Markel Group CEO
description: CEO of Markel Group, the insurance company so closely modeled on Berkshire Hathaway it's nicknamed 'baby Berkshire'. Has run Markel's equity portfolio for over 30 years with a buy-and-hold quality approach, compounding the insurer's float into a multi-billion dollar stock portfolio.

name: Philippe Laffont
title: Coatue Management Founder
description: Founder of Coatue Management and one of the most successful 'Tiger cubs' trained under Julian Robertson. Runs a technology-focused crossover fund investing in both public and private companies, with early conviction bets across AI, consumer internet, and semiconductors.

name: Stephen Mandel
title: Lone Pine Capital Founder
description: Founder of Lone Pine Capital and one of the most respected growth investors of the past three decades. A 'Tiger cub' under Julian Robertson, he built Lone Pine into a powerhouse with deep fundamental research on high-growth franchises across tech, consumer, and healthcare.

name: Andreas Halvorsen
title: Viking Global Founder
description: Norwegian former navy SEAL turned 'Tiger cub' who co-founded Viking Global Investors, one of the largest long/short equity funds in the world. Known for a deep fundamental research process and disciplined portfolio management across public and private markets.

name: Brad Gerstner
title: Altimeter Capital Founder
description: Founder of Altimeter Capital and one of the most vocal technology investors of the AI era. Made concentrated bets on Snowflake and Uber, wrote the famous 'Time to Get Fit' open letter to Meta, and champions the 'Invest America' program to give every American child an investing account.

name: Dan Sundheim
title: D1 Capital Founder
description: Founder of D1 Capital Partners and former chief investment officer of Viking Global. Runs a concentrated crossover portfolio spanning public equities and late-stage private companies, and is regarded as one of the sharpest fundamental analysts of his generation.

name: Duan Yongping
title: H&H International Investment
description: The Chinese electronics billionaire behind BBK, OPPO, and vivo, who retired young and turned to Buffett-style investing. His concentrated portfolio — anchored by an enormous Apple position — has a cult following among value investors, who track his filings as closely as Berkshire's.

name: John Paulson
title: Paulson & Co Founder
description: Architect of 'the greatest trade ever' — his 2007 bet against subprime mortgages via credit default swaps made roughly $15 billion as the housing market collapsed. Later became one of the largest private investors in gold. Now runs Paulson & Co as a family office.

name: Leon Cooperman
title: Omega Family Office Chairman
description: Former CEO of Goldman Sachs Asset Management who founded Omega Advisors and ran it for 27 years before converting to a family office. A classic value investor who files 13Fs under his own name, known for outspoken market commentary and pledging his fortune to charity.

name: Ken Fisher
title: Fisher Investments Founder
description: Founder of Fisher Investments, one of the largest independent wealth managers in the world with well over $200B under management. Wrote the Forbes 'Portfolio Strategy' column for 32 years — the longest run in the magazine's history. His firm's 13F reflects a broad, actively tilted portfolio across thousands of client accounts.

name: Jeremy Grantham
title: GMO Co-Founder
description: Co-founder of asset manager GMO and the market's most famous bubble historian — he identified and sidestepped the Japan bubble, the dot-com bubble, and the 2008 housing bubble. Focuses on long-term asset-class value and has become a leading voice on climate investing.

name: Larry Robbins
title: Glenview Capital Founder
description: Founder of Glenview Capital Management, best known for deep fundamental bets on healthcare and managed-care companies. His 'growth at a reasonable price' hedge fund famously returned over 80% in 2013 on hospital stocks after the Affordable Care Act ruling.

name: Renaissance Technologies
title: Quantitative Hedge Fund
description: Founded by mathematician Jim Simons, Renaissance Technologies is widely considered the most successful quantitative trading firm in history. Their flagship Medallion Fund reportedly generated 66% average annual returns before fees from 1988 to 2018. The firm employs scientists and mathematicians rather than traditional Wall Street analysts.

name: Citadel Advisors
title: Multi-Strategy Hedge Fund
description: Founded by Ken Griffin, Citadel is one of the world's largest and most profitable hedge funds with over $60B in AUM. Operates across multiple strategies including equities, fixed income, commodities, and quantitative trading. Also runs Citadel Securities, one of the largest market makers on Wall Street.

name: Two Sigma Investments
title: Quantitative Hedge Fund
description: A technology-driven systematic investment manager founded by David Siegel and John Overdeck. Uses machine learning, distributed computing, and massive datasets to build predictive models for financial markets. Manages over $60B in assets.

name: Tiger Global Management
title: Technology-Focused Hedge Fund
description: Founded by Chase Coleman III, a protege of legendary investor Julian Robertson at Tiger Management. Became one of the most influential technology investors globally with early bets on companies like Facebook, LinkedIn, and JD.com.

name: D.E. Shaw
title: Quantitative Hedge Fund
description: Founded by David Shaw, D.E. Shaw is one of the largest and most sophisticated quantitative hedge funds in the world, managing over $60B in assets. Combines computational techniques with human judgment across systematic, discretionary, and hybrid strategies spanning equities, fixed income, and alternative investments.

name: Millennium Management
title: Multi-Strategy Hedge Fund
description: Founded by Israel Englander, Millennium Management is one of the world's largest multi-strategy hedge funds managing over $65B in assets. Operates through hundreds of independent trading teams across equities, fixed income, commodities, and quantitative strategies, with rigorous risk controls at both the team and portfolio level.

name: Point72 Asset Management
title: Multi-Strategy Hedge Fund
description: Founded by Steve Cohen, Point72 manages approximately $35B in assets through discretionary long/short equity, systematic trading, and macro strategies. Evolved from SAC Capital Advisors, the firm is known for its deep fundamental research and significant investment in technology and data science.

name: AQR Capital Management
title: Quantitative Investment Manager
description: Founded by Cliff Asness, AQR Capital Management is a global quantitative investment firm managing over $100B in assets. Pioneered factor-based investing strategies including value, momentum, and low-volatility approaches. Known for rigorous academic research and systematic portfolio construction.

name: Harris Associates
title: Oakmark Funds Manager
description: The Chicago value shop behind the Oakmark Funds, long associated with star manager Bill Nygren. Buys businesses trading at significant discounts to intrinsic value and holds through volatility — a classic fundamental value discipline applied at mutual-fund scale.

name: Norges Bank
title: Norwegian Sovereign Wealth Fund
description: Norway's central bank, filing on behalf of the Government Pension Fund Global — the largest sovereign wealth fund on Earth at roughly $1.8 trillion, built from North Sea oil revenue. It owns about 1.5% of every listed company in the world, making its US filings a window into the biggest single pool of equity capital anywhere.

name: Public Investment Fund
title: Saudi Arabia Sovereign Wealth Fund
description: Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund and the financial engine of the kingdom's Vision 2030 transformation. Known for bold concentrated bets — an early $3.5B stake in Uber, majority ownership of Lucid Motors, and headline-grabbing investments across gaming, sports, and technology.

name: Baillie Gifford
title: Long-Horizon Growth Manager
description: The century-old Scottish investment partnership that became famous for finding transformational growth companies years early — it was one of the largest outside investors in Tesla, Amazon, and NVIDIA long before the crowd. Invests with a 5-10 year horizon and asks one question: can this company grow to many times its current size?

name: Nancy Pelosi
title: U.S. Representative (CA-11)
description: Former Speaker of the U.S. House of Representatives, serving California's 11th district since 1987. Her household's stock trades — primarily executed by her husband Paul Pelosi — have drawn significant public attention due to their timing relative to major legislation and regulatory decisions, particularly in the technology sector.

name: Josh Gottheimer
title: U.S. Representative (NJ-5)
description: U.S. Representative for New Jersey's 5th district and one of the most active stock traders in Congress. A moderate Democrat who sits on the Financial Services Committee, his frequent and diversified trading activity across multiple sectors has made him one of the most closely watched congressional traders.

name: Tommy Tuberville
title: U.S. Senator (AL)
description: U.S. Senator from Alabama and former college football coach. One of the most active stock traders in the Senate, frequently trading across technology, energy, and defense sectors. His trading volume and frequency have made him a focal point in debates about congressional stock trading regulations.

name: John Boozman
title: U.S. Senator (AR)
description: U.S. Senator from Arkansas and the most prolific stock trader in the current Senate by transaction count. A senior member of the Appropriations Committee, his portfolio spans hundreds of positions across virtually every sector of the market.

name: Markwayne Mullin
title: U.S. Senator (OK)
description: U.S. Senator from Oklahoma and former professional MMA fighter. Maintains one of the largest and most actively traded portfolios in Congress with positions spanning energy, technology, and industrials.

name: Sheldon Whitehouse
title: U.S. Senator (RI)
description: U.S. Senator from Rhode Island known for his advocacy on climate change and dark money in politics. Despite his reformist stance, maintains an active stock portfolio with frequent trades. Serves on the Senate Budget Committee and Judiciary Committee.

name: Ro Khanna
title: U.S. Representative (CA-17)
description: U.S. Representative for California's 17th district, which encompasses much of Silicon Valley. A progressive Democrat on the Armed Services and Oversight committees. His portfolio reflects his district's tech-centric economy with significant exposure to semiconductor and software companies.

name: Gilbert Cisneros
title: U.S. Representative (CA-38)
description: U.S. Representative for California's 38th district. One of the most active stock traders in the House by transaction volume, with hundreds of trades spanning diverse sectors including technology, healthcare, and financial services.
